basics.name: Ben Duncan
basics.label: AI/ML Engineer | Technical Leader | Philanthropist
basics.summary: AI/ML Engineer specializing in architecting and building production AI systems and deploying complex models at scale.

work.name: Onebrief
work.summary: Architecting and implementing scalable AI services for edge computing and constrained environments.
highlights: Architected multi-agent structured graph workflows and developed Model Context Protocol (MCP) Servers to expose internal application tools for autonomous agent orchestration
Implemented a scalable, modular service for deploying models on Kubernetes in constrained environments
Deployed fine-tuned Large Language Models (LLM) providing AI summarization capabilities
Created machine learning and advanced algorithm services for document layout detection and extraction

work.name: Lockheed Martin
work.summary: Full-lifecycle leadership of MLOps platforms and data-driven product strategy.
highlights: Wore several hats as a product owner and engineer in designing, building, deploying and maintaining data products and ML models in an agile environment
Generated product vision and roadmap, interfaced with users, and led a diverse agile team through concept generation to full product offering
Led the data operations team in data collection, ETL pipelines, and developing data access tools and APIs
Owned ML operations including Kubeflow pipelines, models registry development and model framework tools
Deployed and maintained tools and infrastructure built on Kubernetes and AWS cloud technology

work.name: Lockheed Martin
work.summary: Selective three-year rotational program for high-potential leaders involving cross-disciplinary technical rotations.
highlights: Participated in a three-year rotational program consisting of leadership training, technical training, and the pursuit of a technical Master’s degree.

work.name: Lockheed Martin
work.summary: Driving technical strategy for large-scale data storage and analytics platforms.
highlights: Led an agile scrum team in the development of a TB scale data storage and retrieval platform
Design SQL data catalog for inventorying and organizing terabytes of diverse data assets in object storage
Designed Airflow ETL pipelines for ingesting structured and unstructured data from various sources
Built Python interfaces and tools for querying, storing, and accessing data assets using clean code principle

work.name: Lockheed Martin
work.summary: Facilitating agile delivery of expert systems and telemetry analysis tools.
highlights: Led agile scrum teams, in developing advanced data analysis tools and Expert Systems
Responsible for defining roadmap, backlog grooming, sprint planning and reporting to management
Generated and prepared simulated missile telemetry data for ingestion into Machine Learning pipelines
Leveraged containerization and CI/CD to create a robust development and delivery environment
Developed Python and Tableau tools for data processing, analysis and visualization

work.name: Lockheed Martin
work.summary: Ensuring mission success through rigorous environmental and E3 qualification.
highlights: Led a engineering team through test planning and execution to qualify a missile guidance section
Conducted Environmental and Electromagnetic Environmental Effects (E3) Qualification Testing (MIL-STD-810, MIL-STD-464, MIL-STD-461)
Performed systems integration, systems testing, and requirements verification
Leveraged JIRA KanBan to develop an agile approach to task management, planning and delegation
Navigated complex security environments involving classified assets, facilities and equipment

work.name: Lockheed Martin
work.summary: Designing complex system architectures using Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE).
highlights: Develop detailed MBSE architecture using SysML with Rhapsody
Utilized DOORS and create, capture, link and manage requirements
Developed system level specifications and DoDAF views
Created DXL scripts, VBA tools and procedures to automate repetitive processes

work.name: Lockheed Martin
work.summary: Designing complex system architectures using Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE).
highlights: Worked with domain experts to develop detailed MBSE architecture using SysML with Rhapsody
Utilized DOORS to capture and link requirements, populate attributes, and generate metrics
Developed high-level DoDAF 2.0 views to support customer deliverable artifacts
Regularly prepared and presented architectural content to customers and internal reviewers
Created DXL scripts, VBA tools and procedures to automate repetitive processes

work.name: Lockheed Martin
work.summary: Summer internship focusing on systems architecture and requirements.
highlights: Developed systems architecture and requirements for an advanced mission planning software
Acted as a change agent supporting software and systems teams in transitioning from waterfall to Scrum
Worked directly with engineers and management to develop agile process workflows in Atlassian JIRA
Utilized Rational Rhapsody and DOORS in developing sequence diagrams and systems level requirements
Applied end-user requirements to develop user stories driving sprint planning and roadmap execution

work.name: Gatorloop
work.summary: Founding and leading a multidisciplinary Hyperloop design team at the University of Florida.
highlights: Recruited and managed a multidisciplinary team of 50 in the design and construction of a Hyperloop capsule
Led seven diverse sub-teams and fostered an international partnership with ALES Suspension
Worked with marketing teams and patrons to acquire funding and donations valuing more than $55,000
Developed and executed a realistic project roadmap ultimately meeting defined goals and deadlines
Founded and developed a seedling organization into one of the largest design teams at UF in under a year

work.name: Siemens
work.summary: Rotational internship focusing on technical R&D and product analysis.
highlights: Performed technical R&D on next-generation arc and ground fault circuit interrupting technology
Conducted UL validation testing and non-conformance analysis on profitable product lines
Helped to creatively determine the root cause of a persistent component issue, relieving product holds
Supported tolerance analysis and implemented cost-cutting designs for various DTC projects
Wrote VBA macros and Working Model simulations to aid productivity in prototype development

volunteer.summary: Leading a non-profit organization focused on community fundraising and social impact.
highlights: Leading the local non-profit in planning events and fundraising to support those in need

volunteer.summary: Managing financial operations and budgeting for the organization.
Supporting executive leadership and organizational strategy.
Contributing to strategic planning and event coordination.

references.name: Scott Strauss
references.reference: Never have I come across such a natural leader and valuable team member than Ben. I had the pleasure of having Ben serve as the vice president of our fraternity during my time as president, and time and again Ben proved to be one of our organizations most valuable member. Ben is not only a great leader, but an exceptional friend; one you can trust and rely on for sound advice and council. Ben does not demand respect, but rather people inherently follow him because of his commanding presence and proven track record of excellence. One of Ben's most recognized characteristics is his innate ability to solve problems and improve processes, whether it be in a social, technical, or leadership setting. Ben would be an exceptional addition to any organization, whether in a team or leadership role.
